meta.note: Single source of truth for CV data. The sharp truth: only skills and claims Morten can fully stand behind in conversation. This file feeds the /cv page, the terminal 'cv' command, and later the LinkedIn refresh.
meta.tone: Tone principle: concrete over grandiose. Facts and numbers, no self-praise, no explanations for job changes (curation, not confession). Grades are never shown. Skills kept at a general level.

basics.name: Morten Rode Enghausen
basics.label: Software engineering student | IT infrastructure & operations specialist
basics.email: TODO: hej@enghausen.com once the email migration is done

summary: Software engineering student at Aarhus University with more than a decade of hands-on experience in IT infrastructure, operations and business-critical systems, most recently the electronic health record system serving Central Denmark Region. Trained data technician, thorough and documentation-driven, with a profile spanning servers, networking, automation and process management, now adding formal software engineering on top.

merit.basis: Credits transferred based on vocational experience

education.area: Engineering admission course, Aarhus University School of Engineering
Data Technician specializing in networking and data infrastructure

work.summary: Application team behind MidtEPJ, the electronic health record system (by Systematic) serving ~25,000 daily clinical users.
work.endNote: Resigned to pursue the engineering degree full-time
highlights: Release management for major/minor/hotfix releases across test and production, including final quality control before go-live
Software deployment on a large Citrix cluster and backend operations for all MidtEPJ integrations
Incident and problem manager, with root-cause analysis spanning Java code, servers and end clients
Automation of complex application flows in Perl, PowerShell and Python
ServiceNow development: dashboards and reporting supporting SCRUM-style two-week sprints

work.summary: Founded a semi-professional CS:GO organization competing in POWER Ligaen, Division 3.
highlights: Full operational responsibility: recruitment, team morale, finances, contracts and investments
Built the technical platform with servers and tooling for the entire team

work.summary: Taught e-sports to 7th-9th grade students, evening classes and electives, classes of up to 30 students.
highlights: Responsible for three e-sports locations: workstations, equipment, network and teaching materials
Planned summer and winter camps for up to 40 students
Moved all teaching online during the COVID lockdown while keeping engagement high
Three DGI coaching certifications

work.summary: Own company: an analytics tool turning CS:GO demo data into actionable insights for players and teams.
highlights: Designed and built the analysis tool primarily in Python, containerized with Docker and developed in a GitHub-based workflow
Ran development and test environments across Hetzner, DigitalOcean, AWS and Azure
Reached beta before being discontinued; later an early backer of Leetify, a similar analytics product

work.summary: Classic system administration in an on-premise environment.
highlights: Day-to-day operation of the server room: Windows servers, file services, storage and backup
User and client administration with Active Directory and Group Policy
Same technical foundation as the Danske Fragtmænd years, minus the networking responsibility

work.company: Agency for Governmental IT Services (Statens It)
work.summary: Continued the Danish Agricultural Agency system separation after the consolidation of governmental IT.
highlights: Achieved PRINCE2 Foundation certification
Split the Agriculture and Fisheries Agency IT systems into two separate entities with minimal service disruption

work.summary: Coordinated IT projects and managed problems in close collaboration with IT leadership.
highlights: Bridge between engineering and management on complex IT challenges
Role transitioned to Statens It as part of the governmental IT consolidation

work.position: IT Support Specialist, IT Business Support
work.summary: Operations and monitoring of payment systems in the financial sector.
highlights: Took part in relocating payment systems from Finland to Denmark
Focus on secure and stable card payment operations

work.summary: Continued full-time on the same stack as the apprenticeship: Microsoft roles, Hyper-V, Ubuntu, Atlassian, Cisco/HP networking.

Client management with SCCM, CAPA, Active Directory and Group Policy
